key_insight: Spectral Rewiring for Exploration, Purification, and Model Merging (SAR) isolates the reasoning-relevant spectral core of post-training updates, preserving most gains while removing interference-causing components.
engineering_relevance: SAR is a training-free post-hoc editing method that can improve deployment robustness, reasoning quality, and capability merging for large language models without full retraining.
method: Decompose post-training weight updates in the base model’s spectral space.
Keep the subspace-aligned components that carry reasoning benefits.
Remove orthogonal residual updates that can suppress performance or cause cross-domain interference.
evidence_signal: Across several model families and scales, SAR uses as little as about 0.58% of total parameters, preserves over 99% of post-training performance, improves high-k exploration in math reasoning, and also boosts 6 of 7 agentic coding benchmarks on an in-house model.
watch_item: The method depends on accurately identifying the relevant spectral subspace, so its effectiveness may vary with model architecture, task mixture, and how the update geometry is estimated.
paper_title: Spectral Rewiring for Exploration, Purification, and Model Merging
